title: OT Auditable Event Coverage by Zone and Source

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 SR 2.8 (Auditable events) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.22: OT Auditable Event Coverage by Zone and Source.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.22' running on metadata sources; index=ot_audit, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
| tstats count where index=ot_audit by sourcetype, host prefix=ot
| lookup ot_expected_audit_sources.csv sourcetype OUTPUT zone, required
| eval gap=if(required="true" AND count<100,"low_volume","ok")
| where gap!="ok"
| table sourcetype, host, count, zone
description: Compares expected auditable sources per IEC 62443 zone to actual indexed event rates to find blind spots.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot_audit. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Define required sourcetypes per Purdue level; (2) onboard missing forwarders; (3) validate license throttling not dropping; (4) quarterly coverage attestation. Indexes required: `index=ot_audit`; Lookups: `ot_expected_audit_sources`, `ot_expected_audit_sources.csv`.
visualization: Bar (actual vs expected), Table, Single value (gaps).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for compares expected auditable sources per iec 62443 zone to actual indexed event rates to find blind spots. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `metadata` sources; `index=ot_audit`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Define required sourcetypes per Purdue level; (2) onboard missing forwarders; (3) validate license throttling not dropping; (4) quarterly coverage attestation.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| tstats count where index=ot_audit by sourcetype, host prefix=ot
| lookup ot_expected_audit_sources.csv sourcetype OUTPUT zone, required
| eval gap=if(required="true" AND count<100,"low_volume","ok")
| where gap!="ok"
| table sourcetype, host, count, zone
```

Understanding this SPL

**OT Auditable Event Coverage by Zone and Source (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 2.8)** — Compares expected auditable sources per IEC 62443 zone to actual indexed event rates to find blind spots.

Documented **Data sources**: `metadata` sources; `index=ot_audit`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_audit.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against precomputed summaries; ensure the referenced data model is accelerated.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **gap** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where gap!="ok"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: count, gap, host, sourcetype, zone.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar (actual vs expected), Table, Single value (gaps).
